---
title: "Microeconometrics Using Stata"
Authors:
- Colin Cameron
- Pravin Trivedi
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-expand: true
    toc-title: Contents
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
execute:
  enabled: true
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

[Microeconometrics with R](https://ycroissant.github.io/micsr_book/)

[UCLA](https://stats.oarc.ucla.edu/stata/modules/)

[Github](https://github.com/thousandoaks?tab=repositories)

# Julia setup

```{julia}
using StatEcon
const DATA = let c = filter(d -> isdir(d) && (isfile(joinpath(d, "auto.dta")) || isdir(joinpath(d, "musr"))), ["../../data", "../data", "data"]); isempty(c) ? error("StatEcon example data not found; run this notebook from its own folder (examples/Cameron_Trivedi) or the repo root.") : first(c) end
using DataFrames, ReadStatTables, CSV, Downloads
using Statistics, StatsBase, LinearAlgebra, Printf, Random
using GLM, FixedEffectModels, StatsModels
using CategoricalArrays
using KernelDensity, Loess
using CairoMakie
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "png")
using RegressionTables
import Distributions
using Distributions: cdf, quantile, Normal, TDist, FDist, Chisq

# Small helper: unwrap ReadStatTables.LabeledValue.
_rawval(x) = hasproperty(x, :value) ? x.value : x
```

# 3 Linear regression basics

## 3.1 Introduction

## 3.2 Data and data summary

### 3.2.1 Data description

### 3.2.2 Variable description

```{julia}
#=
set more off
version 11
clear all
set linesize 82
set scheme s1mono  /* Graphics scheme */
=#
# Load medical expenditure dataset
df3 = DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus03data.dta"))
select(df3, [:totexp, :ltotexp, :posexp, :suppins, :phylim, :actlim,
             :totchr, :age, :female, :income]) |> DataFrames.describe
```

### 3.2.3 Summary statistics

```{julia}
#=
* Summary statistics for medical expenditure dataset
summarize totexp ltotexp posexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
=#
# Summary statistics for medical expenditure dataset
DataFrames.describe(df3[:, [:totexp, :ltotexp, :posexp, :suppins, :phylim, :actlim,
                 :totchr, :age, :female, :income]],
         :mean, :std, :min, :max, :nmissing)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Tabulate variable
tabulate income if income <= 0
=#
# Tabulate income <= 0
DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(filter(r -> r.income <= 0, df3), :income), nrow => :freq)
```

### 3.2.4 More-detailed summary statistics

```{julia}
#=
* Detailed summary statistics of a single variable
summarize totexp, detail
=#
# Detailed summary statistics: percentiles, skewness, kurtosis
let x = collect(skipmissing(df3.totexp))
    @printf("N        = %d\n",   length(x))
    @printf("Mean     = %.4f\n", mean(x))
    @printf("Std.Dev. = %.4f\n", std(x))
    @printf("Min      = %.4f\n", minimum(x))
    @printf("p1       = %.4f\n", quantile(x, 0.01))
    @printf("p5       = %.4f\n", quantile(x, 0.05))
    @printf("p25      = %.4f\n", quantile(x, 0.25))
    @printf("p50      = %.4f\n", quantile(x, 0.50))
    @printf("p75      = %.4f\n", quantile(x, 0.75))
    @printf("p95      = %.4f\n", quantile(x, 0.95))
    @printf("p99      = %.4f\n", quantile(x, 0.99))
    @printf("Max      = %.4f\n", maximum(x))
    @printf("Skewness = %.4f\n", skewness(x))
    @printf("Kurtosis = %.4f\n", kurtosis(x) + 3)  # Stata uses non-excess
end
```

```{julia}
stata_summarize(df3, :totexp)
```

### 3.2.5 Tables for data

```{julia}
#=
* Two-way table of frequencies
table female totchr
=#
# Two-way table of frequencies (female × totchr)
DataFrames.unstack(combine(groupby(df3, [:female, :totchr]), nrow => :freq),
        :female, :totchr, :freq)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Two-way table with row and column percentages and Pearson chi-squared
tabulate female suppins, row col chi2
=#
# Two-way table with row/col percentages + chi-squared
ct = DataFrames.unstack(combine(groupby(df3, [:female, :suppins]), nrow => :freq),
             :female, :suppins, :freq)
println("Frequencies:")
println(ct)

# Chi-squared test of independence (manual — avoids Missing-typed matrix)
M_raw = Matrix(ct[:, Not(:female)])
M = Int.(coalesce.(M_raw, 0))           # drop Missing, cast to Int
n  = sum(M)
row_t = sum(M, dims = 2)
col_t = sum(M, dims = 1)
expected = (row_t * col_t) ./ n
χ² = sum((M .- expected).^2 ./ expected)
df_χ = (size(M, 1) - 1) * (size(M, 2) - 1)
@printf("Pearson chi-squared(%d) = %.4f, p = %.4f\n",
        df_χ, χ², 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(df_χ), χ²))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Three-way table of frequencies
table female totchr suppins
=#
# Three-way table of frequencies
DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df3, [:female, :totchr, :suppins]), nrow => :freq)
```

```{julia}
#=
* One-way table of summary statistics
table female, statistic(count totchr) statistic(mean totchr) statistic(sd totchr) statistic(p50 totchr)
=#
# One-way table of summary statistics by female
DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df3, :female),
    :totchr => length    => :count,
    :totchr => mean      => :mean,
    :totchr => std       => :sd,
    :totchr => median    => :p50)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Two-way table of summary statistics
table female suppins, statistic(count totchr) statistic(mean totchr)
=#
# Two-way table of summary statistics
DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df3, [:female, :suppins]),
    :totchr => length => :count,
    :totchr => mean   => :mean)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Summary statistics obtained using command tabstat
tabstat totexp ltotexp, stat (count mean p50 sd skew kurt) col(stat)
=#
# Summary statistics: count, mean, p50, sd, skewness, kurtosis
function tabstat(x)
    xc = collect(skipmissing(x))
    return (count = length(xc),
            mean  = Statistics.mean(xc),
            p50   = Statistics.median(xc),
            sd    = Statistics.std(xc),
            skew  = StatsBase.skewness(xc),
            kurt  = StatsBase.kurtosis(xc) + 3)
end
let s1 = tabstat(df3.totexp), s2 = tabstat(df3.ltotexp)
    DataFrames.DataFrame(
        var   = ["totexp", "ltotexp"],
        count = [s1.count, s2.count],
        mean  = [s1.mean,  s2.mean],
        p50   = [s1.p50,   s2.p50],
        sd    = [s1.sd,    s2.sd],
        skew  = [s1.skew,  s2.skew],
        kurt  = [s1.kurt,  s2.kurt],
    )
end
```

### 3.2.6 Statistical tests

```{julia}
#=
ttest totexp, by(suppins)
=#
# ttest totexp, by(suppins)        -- Stata default: equal-variance (pooled)
stata_ttest(df3, :totexp, :suppins);
```

```{julia}
# ttest totexp, by(suppins) unequal -- Welch's t-test (unequal variances)
stata_ttest(df3, :totexp, :suppins; unequal=true);
```

### 3.2.7 Data plots

```{julia}
#=
* Kernel density plots with adjustment for highly skewed data
kdensity totexp if posexp==1, generate (kx1 kd1) n(500) 
graph twoway (line kd1 kx1) if kx1 < 40000, name(levels)
kdensity ltotexp if posexp==1, generate (kx2 kd2) n(500) 
graph twoway (line kd2 kx2) if kx2 < ln(40000), name(logs)
graph combine levels logs, iscale(1.0)
graph export musr/mus03fig1.eps, replace
=#
# Kernel density plots: levels and logs (for positive expenditures)
pos = df3[df3.posexp .== 1, :]
kd1 = kde(collect(skipmissing(pos.totexp)))
kd2 = kde(collect(skipmissing(pos.ltotexp)))

fig = Figure(size = (900, 400))
ax1 = Axis(fig[1, 1], title = "Density of totexp",  xlabel = "totexp")
ax2 = Axis(fig[1, 2], title = "Density of ltotexp", xlabel = "ln(totexp)")
mask1 = kd1.x .< 40000
mask2 = kd2.x .< log(40000)
lines!(ax1, kd1.x[mask1], kd1.density[mask1])
lines!(ax2, kd2.x[mask2], kd2.density[mask2])
fig
```

## 3.3 Regression in levels and logs

### 3.3.1 Basic regression theory

### 3.3.2 OLS regression and matrix algebra

### 3.3.3 Properties of the OLS estimator

### 3.3.4 Heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors

### 3.3.5 Cluster-robust standard errors

### 3.3.6 Regression in logs

## 3.4 Basic regression analysis

### 3.4.1 Correlations

```{julia}
#=
* Pairwise correlations for dependent variable and regressor variables
correlate ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
=#
# Pairwise correlations
cols = [:ltotexp, :suppins, :phylim, :actlim, :totchr, :age, :female, :income]
df3_clean = dropmissing(df3[:, cols])
cor(Matrix(df3_clean))
```

### 3.4.2 The regress command

```{julia}
#=
* OLS regression with heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors
regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, vce(robust)
=#
# OLS with heteroskedasticity-robust SE
reg1 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3,
    @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + income),
    Vcov.robust())
```

```{julia}
#=
* Display stored results and list available postestimation commands
ereturn list
help regress postestimation
=#
# Stored results
println("R²        = ", StatsBase.r2(reg1))
println("Adj R²    = ", StatsBase.adjr2(reg1))
println("N         = ", StatsBase.nobs(reg1))
println("dof_resid = ", StatsBase.dof_residual(reg1))
println("RSS       = ", GLM.deviance(reg1))
println("MSS       = ", StatsBase.mss(reg1))
```

### 3.4.3 Hypothesis tests

```{julia}
#=
* Wald test of equality of coefficients
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female ///
  income, vce(robust)
test phylim = actlim
=#
# Wald test of equality of coefficients: phylim = actlim
β  = StatsBase.coef(reg1); V = StatsBase.vcov(reg1); cnames = StatsBase.coefnames(reg1)
i_phylim = findfirst(==("phylim"), cnames)
i_actlim = findfirst(==("actlim"), cnames)
diff_est = β[i_phylim] - β[i_actlim]
se_diff  = sqrt(V[i_phylim, i_phylim] + V[i_actlim, i_actlim] -
                2*V[i_phylim, i_actlim])
t_stat   = diff_est / se_diff
@printf("phylim - actlim = %.6f  SE = %.6f  t = %.3f  p = %.4f\n",
        diff_est, se_diff, t_stat,
        2*(1 - cdf(Normal(), abs(t_stat))))
```

```{julia}
#=
*  Joint test of statistical significance of several variables
test phylim actlim totchr
=#
# Joint test of statistical significance of several variables
# test phylim actlim totchr
stata_test(reg1, ["phylim", "actlim", "totchr"]);
```

```{julia}
# Joint Wald test: phylim = actlim = totchr = 0
idx = [findfirst(==(n), cnames) for n in ["phylim","actlim","totchr"]]
βs  = β[idx]; Vs = V[idx, idx]
W = try
    βs' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Vs) * βs
catch
    NaN
end
@printf("Joint W = %.4f, df = %d, p = %.4f\n",
        W, length(idx), 1 - cdf(Chisq(length(idx)), W))
```

```{julia}
# Wald test of equality of coefficients: test phylim = actlim
# regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, vce(robust)
m_wald = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3,
    @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + income),
    Vcov.robust())
β_w  = StatsBase.coef(m_wald)
V_w  = StatsBase.vcov(m_wald)
cn_w = StatsBase.coefnames(m_wald)
i_phy = findfirst(==("phylim"), cn_w)
i_act = findfirst(==("actlim"), cn_w)
# H0: phylim - actlim = 0  →  R = [0 ... 1 ... -1 ... 0], Rβ = β_phy - β_act
diff   = β_w[i_phy] - β_w[i_act]
var_diff = V_w[i_phy, i_phy] + V_w[i_act, i_act] - 2 * V_w[i_phy, i_act]
F_val  = diff^2 / var_diff
df_r   = m_wald.nobs - length(β_w)   # residual df
p_val  = 1 - cdf(FDist(1, df_r), F_val)
@printf(" ( 1)  phylim - actlim = 0\n\n")
@printf("       F(  1, %5d) = %7.2f\n", df_r, F_val)
@printf("            Prob > F = %8.4f\n", p_val)
```

```{julia}
# Joint test of statistical significance of several variables
# test phylim actlim totchr
stata_test(reg1, ["phylim", "actlim", "totchr"]);
```

### 3.4.4 Tables of output from several regressions

```{julia}
#=
* Store and then tabulate results from multiple regressions
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, vce(robust)
estimates store REG1
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female educyr, vce(robust)
estimates store REG2
estimates table REG1 REG2, b(%9.4f) se stats(N r2 F ll) keep(suppins income educyr)
=#
# Store and tabulate results from multiple regressions
REG1 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3, @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + income), Vcov.robust())
REG2 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3, @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + educyr), Vcov.robust())
# estimates table REG1 REG2, b(%9.4f) se stats(N r2 F ll) keep(suppins income educyr)
RegressionTables.regtable(
    REG1, REG2;
    keep = ["suppins", "income", "educyr"],
    regression_statistics = [
        RegressionTables.Nobs,
        RegressionTables.R2,
        RegressionTables.FStat,
        RegressionTables.LogLikelihood])
```

### 3.4.5 Even better tables of regression output

```{julia}
#=
* Tabulate results using user-written command esttab to produce cleaner output
esttab REG1 REG2, b(%10.4f) se scalars(N r2 F ll) mtitles ///
  keep(suppins income educyr) title("Model comparison of REG1-REG2")
=#
# esttab REG1 REG2, b(%10.4f) se scalars(N r2 F ll) mtitles
#   keep(suppins income educyr) title("Model comparison of REG1-REG2")
RegressionTables.regtable(
    REG1, REG2;
    keep = ["suppins", "income", "educyr"],
    labels = Dict(
        "suppins" => "Supplement ins.",
        "income"  => "Income",
        "educyr"  => "Education years"),
    regression_statistics = [
        RegressionTables.Nobs,
        RegressionTables.R2,
        RegressionTables.FStat,
        RegressionTables.LogLikelihood])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Write tabulated results to a file in latex table format
quietly esttab REG1 REG2 using musr/mus03table.tex, replace b(%10.4f) se scalars(N r2 F ll) ///
   mtitles keep(suppins age income educyr _cons) title("Model comparison of REG1-REG2")
=#
# Write tabulated results to a LaTeX file (esttab ... using musr/mus03table.tex)
RegressionTables.regtable(
    REG1, REG2;
    render = RegressionTables.LatexTable(),
    file = "$(DATA)/musr/mus03table.tex",
    keep = ["suppins", "age", "income", "educyr", "(Intercept)"],
    regression_statistics = [
        RegressionTables.Nobs,
        RegressionTables.R2,
        RegressionTables.FStat,
        RegressionTables.LogLikelihood])
println("LaTeX table written to musr/mus03table.tex")
```

```{julia}
#=
* Add a user-calculated statistic to the table
estimates drop REG1 REG2
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female ///
  income, vce(robust)
estadd scalar pvalue = Ftail(e(df_r),e(df_m),e(F))
estimates store REG1
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female ///
  educyr, vce(robust)
estadd scalar pvalue = Ftail(e(df_r),e(df_m),e(F))
estimates store REG2
esttab REG1 REG2, b(%10.4f) se scalars(F pvalue) mtitles keep(suppins) 
=#
# Add a user-calculated statistic (p-value of F-test) to the table
# estadd scalar pvalue = Ftail(e(df_r), e(df_m), e(F))
# Re-fit REG1 and REG2 with robust SEs and compute F p-values
REG1b = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3,
    @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + income),
    Vcov.robust())
REG2b = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3,
    @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + educyr),
    Vcov.robust())
for (label, m) in [("REG1", REG1b), ("REG2", REG2b)]
    fval = m.F
    df_m = length(coef(m)) - 1       # numerator df = number of regressors (excl. intercept)
    df_r = m.nobs - length(coef(m))   # denominator df
    pval = 1 - cdf(Distributions.FDist(df_m, df_r), fval)
    @printf("%s:  F = %.4f,  df_m = %d,  df_r = %d,  p-value = %.6f\n",
            label, fval, df_m, df_r, pval)
end
# Display comparison keeping only suppins, with F and p-value
RegressionTables.regtable(
    REG1b, REG2b;
    keep = ["suppins"],
    regression_statistics = [
        RegressionTables.FStat,
        RegressionTables.FStatPValue])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Factor variables for sets of indicator variables and interactions
regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female c.income ///
 i.famsze c.income#i.famsze, vce(robust) noheader allbaselevels
=#
# Factor variables / interactions: include famsze as categorical and interact with income
df3.famsze_cat = categorical(df3.famsze)
reg_fact = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3,
    @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female +
                       income + famsze_cat + income & famsze_cat),
    Vcov.robust())
```

```{julia}
#=
* Test joint significance of sets of indicator variables and interactions
testparm i.famsz c.income#i.famsze
=#
# Joint test of significance of famsze indicators + interactions
nm = StatsBase.coefnames(reg_fact)
idx_test = findall(n -> occursin("famsze_cat", n), nm)
βt = StatsBase.coef(reg_fact)[idx_test]
Vt = StatsBase.vcov(reg_fact)[idx_test, idx_test]
W = try
    βt' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Vt) * βt
catch
    NaN
end
@printf("W = %.4f, df = %d, p = %.4f\n", W, length(idx_test),
        1 - cdf(Chisq(length(idx_test)), W))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Compute the average marginal effect in model with interactions
quietly regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female c.income ///
 i.famsze c.income#i.famsze, vce(robust) noheader allbaselevels
margins, dydx(income)
=#
# testparm i.famsze c.income#i.famsze
# Joint robust Wald F-test. Build the design matrix manually to avoid
# collinearity issues from formula-based dummy expansion.
df3_tp = dropmissing(df3, [:ltotexp, :suppins, :phylim, :actlim, :totchr,
                            :age, :female, :income, :famsze_cat])

y_tp = Float64.(df3_tp.ltotexp)
# Base regressors (always included)
X_base = hcat(ones(nrow(df3_tp)),
              Float64.(df3_tp.suppins), Float64.(df3_tp.phylim),
              Float64.(df3_tp.actlim), Float64.(df3_tp.totchr),
              Float64.(df3_tp.age),    Float64.(df3_tp.female),
              Float64.(df3_tp.income))
cn_base = ["_cons","suppins","phylim","actlim","totchr","age","female","income"]

# Famsze dummies + income interactions (base level = 1, dropped)
fam_vals = [_rawval(v) for v in df3_tp.famsze_cat]
levs = sort(unique(fam_vals))
base_lev = levs[1]
test_levs = levs[levs .!= base_lev]

X_fam = zeros(nrow(df3_tp), 0)
cn_fam = String[]
# Dummies
for lv in test_levs
    X_fam = hcat(X_fam, Float64.(fam_vals .== lv))
    push!(cn_fam, "$(Int(lv)).famsze")
end
# Interactions income × famsze dummy
inc = Float64.(df3_tp.income)
for lv in test_levs
    col = Float64.(fam_vals .== lv) .* inc
    # Skip if the column is all zeros or has < 2 nonzero values (not estimable)
    if sum(col .!= 0) >= 2
        X_fam = hcat(X_fam, col)
        push!(cn_fam, "$(Int(lv)).famsze#c.income")
    end
end

X_full = hcat(X_base, X_fam)
cn_full = vcat(cn_base, cn_fam)
n_tp = size(X_full, 1); k_tp = size(X_full, 2)

# OLS
β_tp = X_full \ y_tp
u_tp = y_tp - X_full * β_tp

# HC1 robust sandwich
XtXinv = LinearAlgebra.inv(X_full' * X_full)
meat   = X_full' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(u_tp.^2) * X_full
V_hc1  = XtXinv * meat * XtXinv * (n_tp / (n_tp - k_tp))

# Test indices: all famsze-related columns
idx_fam_test = (length(cn_base)+1):k_tp
q    = length(idx_fam_test)
βtp  = β_tp[idx_fam_test]
Vtp  = V_hc1[idx_fam_test, idx_fam_test]
df_r = n_tp - k_tp

F_tp = (βtp' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Vtp) * βtp) / q
p_tp = 1 - cdf(FDist(q, df_r), F_tp)
@printf("testparm i.famsze c.income#i.famsze\n")
for (j, i) in enumerate(idx_fam_test)
    @printf(" (%2d)  %-35s = 0\n", j, cn_full[i])
end
@printf("\n       F(%d, %d) = %7.2f\n", q, df_r, F_tp)
@printf("            Prob > F = %8.4f\n", p_tp)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Compute elasticity for a specified regressor
quietly regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, vce(robust)
margins, eyex(totchr) atmean
=#
# Average marginal effect of income (numerical derivative)
function ame_income(model, df, varname; h = 0.01)
    df_p = copy(df); df_p[!, varname] .= df_p[!, varname] .+ h
    df_m = copy(df); df_m[!, varname] .= df_m[!, varname] .- h
    mean(FixedEffectModels.predict(model, df_p) .- FixedEffectModels.predict(model, df_m)) / (2h)
end
m_lin = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3, @formula(totexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr +
                                   age + female + income), Vcov.robust(); save = :all)
@printf("AME of income = %.4f\n", ame_income(m_lin, df3, :income))
```

```{julia}
# regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female c.income ///
#         i.famsze c.income#i.famsze, vce(robust)
# margins, dydx(income)
#
# With interactions, ∂E[y|X]/∂income = β_income + β_{income×famsze=k_i}
# (the second term is 0 at the base level). AME = mean across the sample.
m_int = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3,
    @formula(totexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female +
                      income + famsze_cat + income & famsze_cat),
    Vcov.robust())
m_int
stata_margins(m_int, df3, :income; kind=:dydx);
```

```{julia}
# Elasticity of totexp w.r.t. totchr at means: (∂y/∂x)·(x̄/ȳ)
β_totchr = StatsBase.coef(m_lin)[findfirst(==("totchr"), StatsBase.coefnames(m_lin))]
ela = β_totchr * mean(skipmissing(df3.totchr)) / mean(skipmissing(df3.totexp))
@printf("Elasticity at means = %.4f\n", ela)
```

```{julia}
# regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, vce(robust)
# margins, eyex(totchr) atmean
stata_margins(m_lin, df3, :totchr; kind=:eyex);
```

## 3.5 Specification analysis

### 3.5.1 Specification tests and model diagnostics

### 3.5.2 Residual diagnostic plots

```{julia}
#=
* Plot of residuals against fitted values
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, ///
  vce(robust)
rvfplot
graph export musr/mus03fig2.eps, replace
=#
# Residuals vs fitted values plot
m_diag = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3, @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr +
                                     age + female + income),
             Vcov.robust(); save = :residuals)
df3_diag = dropmissing(df3[:, [:totexp, :ltotexp, :suppins, :phylim, :actlim, :totchr, :age, :female, :income]])
yhat = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_diag, df3_diag)
uhat = df3_diag.ltotexp .- yhat

fig2 = Figure(size = (700, 500))
ax = Axis(fig2[1, 1], xlabel = "Fitted values", ylabel = "Residuals",
          title = "Residuals vs Fitted")
scatter!(ax, yhat, uhat, markersize = 3)
hlines!(ax, [0], color = :red)
fig2
```

```{julia}
#=
* Details on the outlier residuals
predict uhat, residual
predict yhat, xb
list totexp ltotexp yhat uhat if uhat < -5, clean
=#
# Outlier residuals (uhat < -5)
df3_diag.yhat = yhat
df3_diag.uhat = uhat
outliers = filter(r -> r.uhat < -5, df3_diag)
first(outliers, 10)
```

### 3.5.3 Influential observations

```{julia}
#=
* Compute dfits that combines outliers and leverage
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
predict dfits, dfits
scalar threshold = 2*sqrt((e(df_m)+1)/e(N))
display "dfits threshold = "  %6.3f threshold
tabstat dfits, stat (min p1 p5 p95 p99 max) format(%9.3f) col(stat)
=#
# DFITS-like influence measure (manual via leverage and residuals)
# DFITS_i = t_i * sqrt(h_i / (1 - h_i))
y = df3_diag.ltotexp
X = hcat(ones(nrow(df3_diag)),
         df3_diag.suppins, df3_diag.phylim, df3_diag.actlim,
         df3_diag.totchr, df3_diag.age, df3_diag.female, df3_diag.income)
H = try
    X * LinearAlgebra.inv(X'X) * X'
catch
    # Use pseudoinverse fallback when X'X is singular.
    X * LinearAlgebra.pinv(X'X) * X'
end
h = diag(H)
σ² = sum(uhat.^2) / (length(y) - size(X, 2))
t_resid = uhat ./ sqrt.(σ² .* (1 .- h))
dfits = t_resid .* sqrt.(h ./ (1 .- h))

threshold = 2 * sqrt((size(X, 2)) / nrow(df3_diag))
@printf("DFITS threshold = %.3f\n", threshold)
@printf("min=%.3f  p1=%.3f  p99=%.3f  max=%.3f\n",
        minimum(dfits), quantile(dfits, 0.01),
        quantile(dfits, 0.99), maximum(dfits))
```

```{julia}
#=
list dfits totexp ltotexp yhat uhat if abs(dfits) > 2*threshold & e(sample), clean
=#
# list dfits totexp ltotexp yhat uhat if abs(dfits) > 2*threshold & e(sample)
influential = findall(abs.(dfits) .> 2 * threshold)
if isempty(influential)
    println("No observations exceed 2×threshold = $(round(2*threshold, digits=3))")
else
    listing = DataFrame(
        obs     = influential,
        dfits   = dfits[influential],
        totexp  = df3_diag.totexp[influential],
        ltotexp = df3_diag.ltotexp[influential],
        yhat    = yhat[influential],
        uhat    = uhat[influential])
    sort!(listing, :dfits, by=abs, rev=true)
    listing
end
```

### 3.5.4 Specification tests

#### Test of omitted variables

#### Test of the Box-Cox model

```{julia}
#=
* Boxcox model with lhs variable transformed
boxcox totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income if totexp>0, nolog
=#
# boxcox totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income if totexp>0, nolog
stata_boxcox(df3, :totexp, [:suppins, :phylim, :actlim, :totchr, :age, :female, :income];
           condition = r -> r.totexp > 0)
```


#### Test of the functional form of the conditional mean

```{julia}
#=
* Variable augmentation test of conditional mean using estat ovtest
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female ///
  income, vce(robust)
estat ovtest
=#
# Ramsey RESET test: regress y on X plus powers of fitted values
yhat_diag = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_diag, df3_diag)
df3_diag.yhat2 = yhat_diag.^2
df3_diag.yhat3 = yhat_diag.^3
df3_diag.yhat4 = yhat_diag.^4
m_reset = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3_diag,
    @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female +
                       income + yhat2 + yhat3 + yhat4))
nm = StatsBase.coefnames(m_reset)
idx_p = findall(n -> n in ("yhat2","yhat3","yhat4"), nm)
βp = StatsBase.coef(m_reset)[idx_p]; Vp = StatsBase.vcov(m_reset)[idx_p, idx_p]
F = try
    (βp' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Vp) * βp) / 3
catch
    NaN
end
@printf("RESET F(3, %d) = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
        dof_residual(m_reset), F, 1 - cdf(FDist(3, dof_residual(m_reset)), F))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Link test of functional form of conditional mean 
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female ///
  income, vce(robust)
linktest
=#
# Link test: regress y on yhat and yhat^2
df3_diag.yhat_lt = yhat_diag
m_link = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3_diag, @formula(ltotexp ~ yhat_lt + yhat2))
println(m_link)
```

#### Heteroskedasticity test

```{julia}
#=
* Heteroskedasticity tests using estat hettest and option iid
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
estat hettest, iid
=#
# Breusch–Pagan test: regress squared residuals on regressors
df3_diag.uhat2 = uhat.^2
m_bp = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3_diag, @formula(uhat2 ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr +
                                       age + female + income))
LM = nrow(df3_diag) * StatsBase.r2(m_bp)
@printf("Breusch–Pagan LM = %.3f, df = 7, p = %.4f\n",
        LM, 1 - cdf(Chisq(7), LM))
```


```{julia}
#=
estat hettest suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, iid
=#
# estat hettest, iid
# Breusch-Pagan/Cook-Weisberg test assuming i.i.d. errors, against fitted values
m_het = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3, @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr +
                                   age + female + income))
estat_hettest(m_het, df3; iid=true)
```


```{julia}
# White test: regress squared residuals on X, X² and cross products
# (manual; expand below for selected variables)
m_white = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3_diag, @formula(uhat2 ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr +
                                          age + female + income))
LM_w = nrow(df3_diag) * StatsBase.r2(m_white)
@printf("White-style LM = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", LM_w, 1 - cdf(Chisq(7), LM_w))
```

#### Omnibus test

```{julia}
#=
* Information matrix test
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
estat imtest
=#
# Information matrix test (IM test) — simplified manual version
#println("IM test: see Cameron & Trivedi (2005); manual implementation needed.")
```

```{julia}
# regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
# estat imtest
m_imt = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3,
    @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + income))
estat_imtest(m_imt, df3);
```

### 3.5.5 Tests have power in more than one direction

```{julia}
# Simulation: y = exp(1 + 0.25x + 4x²) + u, fit y on x + x²
Random.seed!(10101)
N = 50
x = rand(N)
u = randn(N)
y = exp.(1 .+ 0.25 .* x .+ 4 .* x.^2) .+ u
xsq = x.^2
df_sim = DataFrame(y = y, x = x, xsq = xsq)
m_sim = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_sim, @formula(y ~ x + xsq))
println(m_sim)
```

```{julia}
# Heteroskedasticity test on the simulated model
yhat_sim = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_sim, df_sim)
uh_sim   = df_sim.y .- yhat_sim
df_sim.uh2 = uh_sim.^2
m_bp_sim = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_sim, @formula(uh2 ~ x + xsq))
LMs = nrow(df_sim) * StatsBase.r2(m_bp_sim)
@printf("BP LM = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", LMs, 1 - cdf(Chisq(2), LMs))
```

```{julia}
# RESET on simulated model
df_sim.yh2 = yhat_sim.^2
df_sim.yh3 = yhat_sim.^3
m_reset_sim = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_sim, @formula(y ~ x + xsq + yh2 + yh3))
nm = StatsBase.coefnames(m_reset_sim)
idx_p = findall(n -> n in ("yh2","yh3"), nm)
βp = StatsBase.coef(m_reset_sim)[idx_p]; Vp = StatsBase.vcov(m_reset_sim)[idx_p, idx_p]
F = try
    (βp' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Vp) * βp) / 2
catch
    NaN
end
@printf("RESET F(2, %d) = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
        dof_residual(m_reset_sim), F,
        1 - cdf(FDist(2, dof_residual(m_reset_sim)), F))
```

## 3.6 Prediction

### 3.6.1 In-sample prediction

```{julia}
#=
* Change dependent variable to level of positive medical expenditures
use musr/mus03data.dta, clear
keep if totexp > 0   
regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, vce(robust)
=#
# Use only positive medical expenditures
df3pos = filter(r -> r.totexp > 0, df3)
m_levels = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3pos,
    @formula(totexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + income),
    Vcov.robust(); save = :all)
println(m_levels)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Prediction in model linear in levels
predict yhatlevels
summarize totexp yhatlevels
=#
# Predicted values vs observed
df3pos_clean = dropmissing(df3pos[:, [:totexp, :suppins, :phylim, :actlim,
                                       :totchr, :age, :female, :income]])
yhat_lev = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_levels, df3pos_clean)
@printf("totexp:     mean = %.2f\n", mean(df3pos_clean.totexp))
@printf("yhatlevels: mean = %.2f\n", mean(yhat_lev))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Compare median prediction and median actual value
tabstat totexp yhatlevels, stat (count p50) col(stat)
=#
# Compare medians
@printf("totexp     median = %.2f  (N=%d)\n",
        median(df3pos_clean.totexp), nrow(df3pos_clean))
@printf("yhatlevels median = %.2f\n", median(yhat_lev))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Compute standard errors of prediction and forecast with default VCE
quietly regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
predict yhatstdp, stdp
predict yhatstdf, stdf
summarize yhatstdp yhatstdf
=#
# Standard errors of prediction (stdp) and forecast (stdf)
X = Matrix(hcat(ones(nrow(df3pos_clean)),
                df3pos_clean.suppins, df3pos_clean.phylim,
                df3pos_clean.actlim,  df3pos_clean.totchr,
                df3pos_clean.age,     df3pos_clean.female,
                df3pos_clean.income))
V_β = StatsBase.vcov(m_levels)
σ²  = sum((df3pos_clean.totexp .- yhat_lev).^2) / dof_residual(m_levels)
stdp = [sqrt(x'V_β*x) for x in eachrow(X)]
stdf = sqrt.(stdp.^2 .+ σ²)
@printf("stdp: mean = %.4f\n", mean(stdp))
@printf("stdf: mean = %.4f\n", mean(stdf))
```


```{julia}
# Compute standard errors of prediction and forecast with default VCE
# regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
# predict yhatstdp, stdp
# predict yhatstdf, stdf
# summarize yhatstdp yhatstdf
xvars_pred = [:suppins, :phylim, :actlim, :totchr, :age, :female, :income]
df3_pred = dropmissing(df3[:, vcat([:totexp], xvars_pred)])
# Unwrap any LabeledValue types and convert to Float64
for c in vcat([:totexp], xvars_pred)
    df3_pred[!, c] = Float64.([_rawval(v) for v in df3_pred[!, c]])
end
@printf("N = %d\n", nrow(df3_pred))

m_pred = GLM.lm(@formula(totexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + income),
                df3_pred)
Xm_pred = GLM.modelmatrix(m_pred)
n_pred  = nrow(df3_pred)
k_pred  = size(Xm_pred, 2)
yhat_p  = GLM.predict(m_pred)
e_pred  = Float64.(df3_pred.totexp) .- yhat_p
sig2_ols = sum(e_pred.^2) / (n_pred - k_pred)         # σ̂² = RSS/(n-k)
XtXinv  = LinearAlgebra.inv(Xm_pred' * Xm_pred)       # (X'X)⁻¹

# stdp = SE of fitted value = σ̂ · sqrt(xᵢ'(X'X)⁻¹xᵢ)
# stdf = SE of forecast     = sqrt(σ̂² + σ̂² · xᵢ'(X'X)⁻¹xᵢ) = σ̂ · sqrt(1 + xᵢ'(X'X)⁻¹xᵢ)
yhatstdp = [sqrt(sig2_ols * dot(x, XtXinv * x)) for x in eachrow(Xm_pred)]
yhatstdf = [sqrt(sig2_ols * (1.0 + dot(x, XtXinv * x))) for x in eachrow(Xm_pred)]

@printf("Root MSE = %.4f\n", sqrt(sig2_ols))
println("    Variable |        Obs        Mean    Std. dev.       Min        Max")
println("-------------+---------------------------------------------------------")
@printf("    yhatstdp | %10d  %10.1f  %10.4f  %10.4f  %10.3f\n",
        n_pred, mean(yhatstdp), std(yhatstdp), minimum(yhatstdp), maximum(yhatstdp))
@printf("    yhatstdf | %10d  %10.2f  %10.5f  %10.2f  %10.1f\n",
        n_pred, mean(yhatstdf), std(yhatstdf), minimum(yhatstdf), maximum(yhatstdf))
```


### 3.6.2 Marginal effects

```{julia}
#=
* Compute elasticity for a specified regressor
quietly regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income, vce (robust)
=#
# In a linear model, ME of regressor j = β_j; elasticity at means = β_j*x̄/ȳ
β_totchr = StatsBase.coef(m_levels)[findfirst(==("totchr"), StatsBase.coefnames(m_levels))]
@printf("Elasticity of totexp w.r.t. totchr (at means) = %.4f\n",
        β_totchr * mean(df3pos_clean.totchr) / mean(df3pos_clean.totexp))
```

### 3.6.3 Prediction in logs: The retransformation problem

```{julia}
#=
* Prediction in levels from a logarithmic model
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
quietly predict lyhat
generate yhatwrong = exp(lyhat)
generate yhatnormal = exp(lyhat)*exp(0.5*e(rmse)^2)
quietly predict uhat, residual
generate expuhat = exp(uhat)
quietly summarize expuhat
generate yhatduan = r(mean)*exp(lyhat) 
summarize totexp yhatwrong yhatnormal yhatduan yhatlevels
=#
# Log model: prediction in levels using normal vs Duan smearing
m_log = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3pos, @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr +
                                       age + female + income); save = :all)
df3pos_log = dropmissing(df3pos[:, [:totexp, :ltotexp, :suppins, :phylim,
                                     :actlim, :totchr, :age, :female, :income]])
lyhat = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_log, df3pos_log)
uhat  = df3pos_log.ltotexp .- lyhat
σ²    = sum(uhat.^2) / dof_residual(m_log)

yhatwrong  = exp.(lyhat)
yhatnormal = exp.(lyhat) .* exp(0.5 * σ²)        # log-normal correction
yhatduan   = mean(exp.(uhat)) .* exp.(lyhat)     # Duan smearing
@printf("totexp     mean = %.2f\n", mean(df3pos_log.totexp))
@printf("yhatwrong  mean = %.2f\n", mean(yhatwrong))
@printf("yhatnormal mean = %.2f\n", mean(yhatnormal))
@printf("yhatduan   mean = %.2f\n", mean(yhatduan))
```

```{julia}
stata_summarize(df3pos_log, [:totexp])
```

### 3.6.4 Prediction exercise

```{julia}
#=
* Predicted effect of supplementary insurance: methods 1 and 2
bysort suppins: summarize totexp yhatlevels yhatduan
=#
# Predicted effect of supplementary insurance: by-group means
df3pos_log.yhatnormal = yhatnormal
df3pos_log.yhatduan   = yhatduan
combine(groupby(df3pos_log, :suppins),
    :totexp     => mean,
    :yhatnormal => mean,
    :yhatduan   => mean)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Predicted effect of supplementary insurance: method 3 for log-linear model
quietly regress ltotexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income
preserve
quietly replace suppins = 1
quietly predict lyhat1
generate yhatnormal1 = exp(lyhat1)*exp(0.5*e(rmse)^2) 
quietly replace suppins = 0
quietly predict lyhat0 
generate yhatnormal0 = exp(lyhat0)*exp(0.5*e(rmse)^2) 
generate treateffect = yhatnormal1 - yhatnormal0
summarize yhatnormal1 yhatnormal0 treateffect 
restore
=#
# Treatment effect via counterfactual prediction (suppins=1 vs 0)
df1 = copy(df3pos_log); df1.suppins .= 1
df0 = copy(df3pos_log); df0.suppins .= 0
ly1 = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_log, df1)
ly0 = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_log, df0)
yh1 = exp.(ly1) .* exp(0.5 * σ²)
yh0 = exp.(ly0) .* exp(0.5 * σ²)
@printf("E[y|x,suppins=1] = %.2f\n", mean(yh1))
@printf("E[y|x,suppins=0] = %.2f\n", mean(yh0))
@printf("Treatment effect = %.2f\n", mean(yh1 .- yh0))
```

## 3.7 Sampling weights

### 3.7.1 Weights

```{julia}
#=
* Create artificial sampling weights
use musr/mus03data.dta, clear
generate swght = totchr^2 + 0.5
summarize swght
=#
# Construct artificial sampling weights
df3 = DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus03data.dta"))
df3.swght = df3.totchr.^2 .+ 0.5
@printf("swght: mean = %.4f, sd = %.4f\n",
        mean(skipmissing(df3.swght)), std(skipmissing(df3.swght)))
```

### 3.7.2 Weighted mean

```{julia}
#=
* Calculate the weighted mean
mean totexp [pweight=swght]
=#
# Weighted mean of totexp
let df = dropmissing(df3[:, [:totexp, :swght]])
    @printf("Weighted mean of totexp = %.4f\n",
            sum(df.totexp .* df.swght) / sum(df.swght))
end
```

### 3.7.3 Weighted regression

```{julia}
#=
* Perform weighted regression 
regress totexp suppins phylim actlim totchr age female income [pweight=swght]
=#
# Weighted regression
m_w = FixedEffectModels.reg(df3,
    @formula(totexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age + female + income),
    weights = :swght, Vcov.robust())
```

### 3.7.4 Weighted prediction and MEs

```{julia}
#=
* Weighted prediction
quietly predict yhatwols
mean yhatwols [pweight=swght], noheader 
=#
# Weighted prediction (mean of yhat using weights)
df3_w = dropmissing(df3[:, [:totexp, :suppins, :phylim, :actlim, :totchr,
                             :age, :female, :income, :swght]])
yhat_w = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_w, df3_w)
@printf("Weighted   mean of yhatwols = %.4f\n",
        sum(yhat_w .* df3_w.swght) / sum(df3_w.swght))
@printf("Unweighted mean of yhatwols = %.4f\n", mean(yhat_w))
```

## 3.8 OLS using native Julia (matrix algebra)

```{julia}
# OLS with White robust SE — native Julia, equivalent to the Mata example
df3pos = filter(r -> r.totexp > 0, df3)
df3p   = dropmissing(df3pos[:, [:ltotexp, :suppins, :phylim, :actlim,
                                 :totchr, :age, :female, :income]])
y = df3p.ltotexp
X = Matrix(hcat(df3p.suppins, df3p.phylim, df3p.actlim, df3p.totchr,
                df3p.age, df3p.female, df3p.income, ones(nrow(df3p))))
xnames = ["suppins","phylim","actlim","totchr","age","female","income","_cons"]

n, k    = size(X)
XtX_inv = try
    LinearAlgebra.inv(LinearAlgebra.cholesky(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(X'X)))
catch
    LinearAlgebra.pinv(X'X)  # Use pseudoinverse for singular matrices
end
b       = XtX_inv * X' * y
e       = y .- X*b
s2      = (e'e) / (n - k)
V_def   = s2 .* XtX_inv                                     # default VCE (unused)
V_white = XtX_inv * (X' * Diagonal(e.^2) * X) * XtX_inv * (n / (n - k))   # robust

se = sqrt.(diag(V_white))
println("─" ^ 70)
@printf("%-10s %12s %12s %8s %8s\n", "", "Coef.", "Rob.SE", "t", "P>|t|")
println("─" ^ 70)
for i in 1:k
    t = b[i] / se[i]
    p = 2 * (1 - cdf(Normal(), abs(t)))
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f %12.6f %8.2f %8.4f\n", xnames[i], b[i], se[i], t, p)
end
println("─" ^ 70)
@printf("N = %d, k = %d, R² = %.4f\n",
        n, k, 1 - sum(e.^2)/sum((y .- mean(y)).^2))
```

```{julia}
# Cross-check with FixedEffectModels.jl
reg(df3pos, @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + phylim + actlim + totchr + age +
                                female + income), Vcov.robust())
```

## 3.9 Stata resources

## 3.10 Exercises
